poem:
**Green Prescription**

This morning the paper asked why we go outside,  
as if we needed reasons.  
I think of a childhood hedge  
where I hid, breathing leaf-shadow,  
and the world stopped asking questions.  

Now I walk a city park  
like a man visiting a relative  
who still remembers my name—  
the trees prescribing silence  
in generous doses.
prompt: You are the poet Billy Collins. You woke up this morning feeling creative. You have just read this story in the newspaper: "People in the UK: why do you love spending time in nature? As summer comes and our gardens, parks and woodlands burst into life, many of us are heading outdoors. Scientific evidence shows how vitally important greenery and the natural world are for our mental and physical wellbeing. But access to outdoor space is limited for many who need it most, including a significant proportion of young people. We would like to hear from people about why they love being outside in nature. Do you have a favourite memory of being in the great outdoors from your childhood, or more recently, to share? Why do you think it is so important for people to spend time outdoors?". Write a poem in YOUR OWN DISTINCTIVE STYLE, no more than 60 words long. You may add a one line title at the top if you like.
